target.name: Verkor
target.founder: Benoit Lemaignan, Christophe Mille, Sylvain Paineau, Philippe Chain, Gilles Moreau, Olivier Dufour

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $2.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: KoBold Metals
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $2B+

private.name: Electric Hydrogen
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B+

private.name: Amogy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $700M

data.narrative:
Verkor is a Series C-stage climate and energy company that has raised over €3B to date. The valuation assessment draws on a peer universe of 9 private companies and 1 public comparable (Linamar, ticker LNZA) operating in the Climate & Energy sector.

The analysis applies peer-median triangulation based on three same-sector, same-stage private comparables: KoBold Metals ($2.0B), Electric Hydrogen ($1.0B), and Amogy ($700M). The median valuation across this set is $1.0B, with the range spanning $700M to $2.0B. This method yielded an implied valuation of $1.0B for Verkor. While a broader comp set of 9 private peers exists (including TAE Technologies and Redwood Materials), the core triangulation relies on the three closest-match Series C stage companies.

The triangulated valuation range is $850M (low), $1.0B (median), and $1.1B (high). The relatively narrow band reflects the consistency of same-stage peer valuations. Key uncertainties include the limited number of applicable comparable transactions at Series C in this sector, the heterogeneity of underlying technology and capital intensity across climate tech verticals, and the timing sensitivity of early-stage energy infrastructure valuations. A single triangulation method constrains confidence; additional data on revenue multiples, capital efficiency, or deal flow timing would strengthen the analysis.
